title: Draw core Latin glyph set (UFO source, GF Latin Core + accessibility differentiation set)
context: With the design brief (fonts-design-101) and build pipeline (fonts-infra-001) in place, M1 requires the actual glyph drawing work. This is the craft-heavy core of the M1 milestone: drawing the accessibility-first Latin family in UFO format, covering GF Latin Core plus the accessibility-critical differentiation set, with a variable weight axis. Slow, unforgiving work; quality is non-negotiable.
objective: Draw the core Latin glyph set as a clean-room original in UFO 3 + designspace, covering GF Latin Core and the accessibility-critical differentiation set (I/l/1, O/0, b/d/p/q, rn/m) with distinct, non-confusable letterforms, a variable weight axis, and spacing/kerning reviewed, building cleanly through the M0 pipeline with FontBakery green.
acceptanceCriteria: UFO source covers GF Latin Core plus the accessibility differentiation set with distinct, non-confusable forms for I/l/1, O/0, b/d/p/q, rn/m, and related confusable pairs.
Builds cleanly through the M0 pipeline (fontmake + gftools + fontTools); FontBakery Universal and GF profiles pass with no FAIL; WARNs triaged and documented.
Variable weight axis builds correctly; static weight instances export as OTF and TTF without errors.
Spacing and kerning have been reviewed; no unintended collisions or gaps at representative type sizes.
provenance.json records every glyph source as original clean-room or OFL/PD-derived with attribution; no proprietary derivation.
All drawings are original clean-room work or explicitly documented OFL/PD-derived forms; no tracing or autotracing from proprietary fonts.
output: A pull request adding the UFO source, designspace, and built OTF/TTF/variable/WOFF2 artifacts for the accessibility-first Latin family, with provenance.json, passing FontBakery, and a type designer's review note.
